title: Autoresearch over a Collective + Distributed Candidate Pool — M4 Air, val_bpb=1.9263 (non-record)
author: AnirudhaRamesh
architecture: Transformer with SwiGLU MLP and mirrored recurrence

data.description: Replaced relu² with gated SiLU activation (proj(silu(gate(x)) * fc(x))) for consistent bpb improvement
4 unique transformer blocks reused across 8 logical layers with encoder forward and decoder reverse passes with U-Net skip connections, halving parameter count while maintaining effective depth
Gradient clipping with norm 1.0 for training stability with SwiGLU
Memory management optimizations including setting MXNet cache limit to 2GB and clearing cache after optimizer steps to fit 16GB M4 Air memory constraints

novel_contributions: Distributed autoresearch with an evolutionary bandit over a collective candidate pool
Periodic scanning and incorporation of new Mac-relevant PR candidates into the candidate pool for iterative improvement
Use of mirrored recurrence architecture to halve parameter count while maintaining effective depth, enabling more training steps within strict memory and time constraints
Optimization of training batch size and sequence length to maximize training steps within a 10-minute wallclock budget on memory-limited Apple M4 Air
Combination of SwiGLU activation and mirrored recurrence with gradient clipping for breakthrough performance improvements
Creation of a distributed iterative improvement ecosystem where improved candidates can be shared and used as seeds by others
Mac-specific fixes including warmdown schedule correction and memory management tailored for Apple Silicon constraints
